subject: infra(deploy): migrate from GitHub Pages to Vercel
body:
Replace GitHub Pages deployment with Vercel:
- Add vercel.json with SPA rewrites (replaces 404.html routing hack)
- Delete .github/workflows/deploy.yml (no longer needed)
- Delete public/404.html (Vercel handles SPA routing natively)
- Remove base path '/four-ems/' from vite.config.ts (Vercel serves at root)
- Update PWA scope and start_url to '/'
- Remove BrowserRouter basename="/four-ems" from main.tsx
- Remove SPA route-restore script from index.html
- Update CLAUDE.md deployment docs

Why: GitHub Pages has friction for SPAs — wrong branch triggers, no env var
injection at build, and the 404.html copy hack for client-side routing.
Vercel handles all of this out of the box.

Alternatives considered:
- Stay on GitHub Pages: Rejected — more maintenance, no env var injection
- Netlify: Rejected — backend already on Vercel, simpler to keep one platform

Tags: infrastructure, deployment, vercel
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: vercel-migration
Semver: major

title: migrate from GitHub Pages to Vercel
